US , Iran Sign Interim Deal to End Hostilities , Ease Sanctions ; Nuclear Talks to Continue
US Signs Deal With Iran, But Some Trump Allies Not Happy
items.summary: President Donald Trump signed an interim deal to end the war with Iran and reopen the Strait of Hormuz, but some Republicans say the US is giving up too much. Laura Davison reports on how the deal came together and what comes next.  (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: US-Iran deal: technical work can begin, says atomic energy agency
items.summary: The head of the UN’s atomic energy agency on Thursday welcomed the signing of an initial Iran-US memorandum aimed at ending the war, before proposing “to sit down” with both parties to assist with concrete measures such as verification of Iran’s nuclear programme, a key sticking point.

items.title: Wellington’s Geary Says Stocks Rotation Trade Is Coming Back
items.summary: The end of the Iran war is reviving investor appetite for stocks outside the US, according to Scott Geary, the head of global wealth at Wellington Management.

items.title: South Africa Impeachment Panel to Oppose President’s Interdict
items.summary: A panel of South African lawmakers that is conducting an inquiry into whether President Cyril Ramaphosa should be impeached over his handling of a robbery at his game farm said it will oppose his application to halt their work.

💬 #Zakharova: June 22 marks 85 years since the onset of the Great Patriotic War, a date forever etched in our national memory and history of humanity.

It lasted 1418 days and nights, claimed 27 mln Soviet lives, and left its mark on every single family across the nation.

items.summary: India’s central bank approved the re-appointment of Keki Mistry as part-time chairman of HDFC Bank Ltd., months after the abrupt resignation of his predecessor sparked questions about governance issues at India’s largest private-sector lender.

items.title:
With Prime Minister of Belgium @Bart_DeWever, we had a detailed discussion about support for our defense. Belgium is preparing a substantial new aid package and a contribution to the PURL initiative. We also went over the delivery schedule for F-16 fighter jets. The first aircraft will arrive this year. This is exactly the kind of support for protecting our skies that we need most right now. Thank you!

I briefed the Prime Minister on our meetings and agreements at the G7 Summit, as well as on our diplomatic engagements aimed at ending this Russian war. It is truly important that pressure on the aggressor continues to increase. We also discussed Ukraine’s European integration. I thank Belgium – the Government and all the people – for their support.

items.title: North Korea warns of ‘disaster’ if G7 tries to strip its nuclear weapons

North Korea’s Kim ⁠Yo-jong, the powerful sister of leader Kim Jong-un, condemned a G7 call for the country’s denuclearisation as a violation of its constitution and an infringement of ‌sovereignty, state media KCNA said on Thursday.
